issues.criterion: ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 4's per-item revision/timestamp anchoring rule ('a baseline MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions') and design_notes.baseline_field_synthesis's own claims that the CLI 'derives all six [baseline categories] deterministically from local, inspectable state, never fabricating an unlabelled value', that 'framework_references and repository_references both anchor to --repo-root's own `git rev-parse HEAD`', and that ci_references carries 'an explicit, honest additionalProperties note field stating no externally-verified CI run is available' (a documented KNOWN LIMITATION, not a silent fabrication).
issues.evidence: Built an adversarial mutant CLI (scratch: mutants/mutant_fabricated.py) that implements the blocking/exception/exit-code mechanics HONESTLY and identically to a correct implementation, but whose baseline.json content is fully fabricated: repository_references/framework_references/planning_references/evidence_references all carry a hardcoded, unrelated 40-hex SHA ('cafef00d...') instead of the fixture's real `git rev-parse HEAD`, all timestamps are a hardcoded '2020-01-01T00:00:00Z' unrelated to the run's actual clock read, and the ci_references item OMITS the promised honest 'note' entirely, instead asserting a fabricated 'run_id': 'gh-actions-run-99999999' and 'status': 'verified-green' as if a real, externally-verified CI run had been checked. Ran all 22 of this contract's own verification commands, verbatim and in order, against this mutant on a disposable `git clone` of the real repository at HEAD 17408d942cb09acced7c0874cd4bd363dce3bfc5 (.../scratchpad/041review/clone1): ALL 22 ENTRIES PASS (exit 0), including entry 5 (self-fixture schema validation) and entry 11 (approved-fixture schema validation), the only two entries that jsonschema.validate() baseline.json at all. Root cause: schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json's $defs/reference_item only requires non-empty STRING 'revision' and 'timestamp' (additionalProperties: true) -- it validates shape, never truth -- and no verification entry in this contract independently recomputes the fixture's actual git HEAD (or the actual run clock) and compares it against the emitted baseline field values.
issues.required_fix: Add at least one verification entry (or extend an existing one, e.g. entry 5's self-fixture run or entry 12's clean-PASS fixture) that independently recomputes the target repo-root's real `git -C <repo-root> rev-parse HEAD` and asserts baseline.json's repository_references[0]['revision'] (and framework_references[0]['revision']) equals it exactly -- not merely that the key is present and non-empty; and asserts the ci_references item carries the honest 'note' key (or an equivalent, structurally pinned honesty marker) design_notes promises, so a CLI that fabricates a differing SHA, a stale/unrelated timestamp, or a false 'verified' CI claim is caught by the suite rather than merely passing schema-shape validation.

missing_acceptance_coverage: No verification entry confirms that any emitted baseline.json field's revision/timestamp VALUE matches the real, independently-observable state of the fixture or self-fixture repository (actual `git rev-parse HEAD`, actual capture-time clock) -- only field presence and non-empty-string shape are checked (entries 5, 11, 12), which is the acceptance-relevant gap ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 4's anchoring rule implies but this contract does not test.
unsupported_claims: design_notes.baseline_field_synthesis: 'framework_references and repository_references both anchor to --repo-root's own `git rev-parse HEAD`' -- no verification entry recomputes and compares against the real HEAD.

design_notes.baseline_field_synthesis: ci_references 'anchors to repo HEAD with an explicit, HONEST additionalProperties note field stating no externally-verified CI run is available in this offline/self-fixture mode' -- no verification entry asserts this field's presence or content.

design_notes.baseline_field_synthesis: the CLI 'never fabricat[es] an unlabelled value' -- not verifiable from the contract's own 22-entry verification array as currently written; presently a documentation-only assertion.
observations: KILL-SHOT CONFIRMED (probe 1, always-exit-0/copied-fixture mutant): built mutants/mutant_always_pass.py (argparse-shaped, documents exit codes so entries 1-4/16-22 pass identically to an honest implementation, but ALWAYS writes the SAME hardcoded PASS preflight.json + a copied-fixture baseline.json and exits 0 regardless of --repo-root's actual state). Ran all 22 verification commands verbatim against it on the disposable clone: entries 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, and 15 (all six behavior-fixture entries) correctly FAIL (rc=1) -- e.g. entry 7's dirty-tracked fixture reports 'expected exit 1, got 0'; entry 10's pending fixture reports 'expected exit 2, got 0'. Entries 12 (clean-PASS fixture) and 13 (determinism) both still PASS against this mutant, which is EXPECTED and worth flagging (see next observation) rather than a defect: a hardcoded output is trivially both 'PASS on a clean fixture' and 'deterministic'.

DETERMINISM ENTRY IS NOT, ALONE, A DISCRIMINATOR AGAINST A FIXTURE-COPYING MUTANT: entry 13 (two runs, same fixture, same verdict) passes identically for both an honest implementation and the always-PASS mutant above, since a hardcoded value is trivially repeatable. The suite's actual defense against that mutant class is entirely carried by the six behavior-fixture entries (7-11, 15), which independently and correctly kill it by forcing DIVERGENT ground-truth scenarios (dirty/untracked/missing-ref/pending/approved/precedence) that a hardcoded responder cannot satisfy. This is fine as constructed (redundant, correctly-attributed coverage) -- flagged only so a future contract author does not mistake 'determinism' for a semantic-correctness check in isolation.

PROBE 2 CONFIRMED, ENTRY 7 IS THE CATCH: built mutants/mutant_dirty_bypass.py, identical to an honest reference implementation (mutants/base_honest.py, independently authored from design_notes and confirmed to pass all 22 entries cleanly as a sanity baseline) except the tracked-dirty-file blocking check is silently dropped (only untracked files are ever blocking). Entry 7's dedicated dirty-tracked-file fixture correctly FAILS ('expected exit 1, got 0'); entry 15's combined precedence fixture also independently FAILS on the same mutant ('expected FAIL (exit 1) to win over exceptions+approval, got 3'). Entries 8, 9, 12, 13 do not detect this mutant (as expected -- they don't exercise a dirty tracked file), confirming entry 7 is the correctly-targeted, load-bearing check for this defect class.

PROBE 3 CONFIRMED, ENTRY 15 IS THE *SOLE* CATCH (single point of failure for this defect class, flagged for awareness, not blocking): built mutants/mutant_precedence.py -- identical to the honest reference except that whenever ANY --tolerate-untracked path is supplied at all, EVERY blocking finding (dirty tracked files, missing references, everything, not just the specific declared-tolerated untracked path) is downgraded into a non-blocking exception. Ran entries 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13 against this mutant: ALL SEVEN PASS (rc=0) -- none of them detect the precedence violation, because none of those fixtures combine an actual blocking condition with a --tolerate-untracked flag in the same invocation. ONLY entry 15 (the dirty+tolerated-untracked+operator-approval combined fixture) correctly FAILS ('expected FAIL (exit 1) to win over exceptions+approval, got 3'). This proves entry 15 is not merely 'defense in depth' as its own acceptance_test text implies, but is presently the ONLY verification entry in the 22-entry suite capable of catching this entire defect class; if entry 15 were ever weakened, reworded to no longer combine all three conditions, or removed in a future revision, this precedence-violation class would ship completely undetected by the remaining 21 entries. Non-blocking because entry 15 exists today, is well-constructed, and is exactly the check design_notes cites for this purpose -- flagged for awareness only.

PROBE 5 CONFIRMED, ENTRY 10'S EXISTENCE-GATE IS ROBUST AGAINST SCHEMA-VALID FORGERY: built mutants/mutant_smuggle_pending.py -- on the unapproved-exceptions ('pending') path, instead of withholding preflight.json and writing only the intentionally non-conformant preflight.pending.json (the honest, contracted design), this mutant writes preflight.json directly with a FABRICATED, schema-VALID operator_approval block ('auto-generated' approver/authorization the operator never actually supplied) while still exiting 2. Entry 10 correctly FAILS this mutant ('an unapproved PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS must not write the final schema-gated artifact') -- because entry 10's check gates on preflight.json's mere EXISTENCE (`test -f "$OUT/preflight.json" && exit 1`), not on validating its content, a schema-valid forgery is caught exactly as reliably as a schema-invalid one design_notes originally envisioned. This is a stronger result than the design_notes' own stated dry-run proof claims (which only demonstrated the informational preflight.pending.json fails schema validation) -- entry 10 additionally, and independently, defeats a more sophisticated smuggling attempt design_notes did not explicitly anticipate.

PROBE 6 CONFIRMED (usage-error and missing-reference discrimination): mutants/mutant_usage_error_wrong_code.py (usage errors fall through to argparse's own default exit 2 instead of the contracted 64) is correctly caught by entry 14 ('expected exit 64 on a missing required argument, got 2'). mutants/mutant_partial_ref_check.py (checks only schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, silently omitting schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json from the required-reference sweep) is correctly caught by entry 9's dual-path assertion (AssertionError naming the omitted 'ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' path specifically).

BONUS PROBE, OUTPUT ISOLATION (entry 6) CONFIRMED NON-VACUOUS: built mutants/mutant_side_effect.py -- an otherwise-honest implementation that additionally leaks a stray 'collection-log.txt' write into --repo-root instead of --output-dir. Entry 6 correctly FAILS ('CLI run altered the repository tree's git status', printing the BEFORE/AFTER diff naming the leaked file) -- confirms this entry is a genuine behavioral check, not a vacuous no-op, since the fixture-copying mutant (probe 1) which writes nothing into repo-root still passed it correctly.

NDEBT-014 SWEEP: (1) HEAD-anchored anti-vacuity -- entry 2 uses `git cat-file -e HEAD:tools/ecosystem_preflight.py`, correctly avoiding catalogued defect class 1 (a working-tree-anchored anti-vacuity check that becomes mutually exclusive with its own positive companion post-implementation). (2) Two-part .agent immutability invariant -- entry 21 follows the 038-amendment/040-precedent harmonized form (tracked-history diff-clean + untracked-addition confinement to a glob range, here 034-042) rather than a fragile per-feature-ID exclude list (catalogued defect class 2); live-tested by adding a synthetic out-of-range artifact (.agent/qa/050.json) to the disposable clone -- entry 21 correctly rejected it ('out-of-scope untracked .agent/qa or .agent/contracts artifact: .agent/qa/050.json'). (3) vlib_section_grep line-wrap fragility -- not applicable; confirmed by direct search that none of this contract's 22 commands invoke vlib_section_grep (this feature edits no markdown protocol prose). (4) Bare substring false-pass -- manually audited entry 4's five regex rows; each anchors a `\b` word boundary immediately after its numeric exit code and requires the corresponding literal enum/keyword immediately after via `.*`, with no containing-word overlap identified (e.g. the '1' row cannot match inside '10' or '100' since `\b` fails between two word characters). (5) Natural-language paraphrase-mirroring -- not applicable; entry 4's docstring check is a structural, numeric/enum-anchored regex sweep, not an open natural-language content bound.

MODE A NOTE ASSESSMENT (independent, test-execution perspective): this evaluator could not locate a persisted Mode A verdict artifact for feature 041 on disk -- .agent/validator/ presently holds only 026 through 030's mode-a/mode-b pairs, no 041-mode-a.json -- and so could not re-read Mode A's exact three NOTEs verbatim per AH-1; this assessment proceeds from the orchestrator's characterization of their subject matter only. On the 'pending-withholding design' NOTE: independently and robustly confirmed correct and even more robust than design_notes' own dry-run claims (see the entry-10 smuggling result above). On the 'ci_references honest-note' NOTE: independent adversarial testing shows this is not merely a stylistic awareness point but the surface of a real, currently-unguarded verification gap (see the blocking issue above) -- this evaluator elevates it from an awareness NOTE to a required pre-implementation fix, since it is directly demonstrated to be exploitable by a schema-valid, fully fabricated CI claim that no entry in the 22-entry suite catches. Recommend the validator's Mode A output be durably written to `.agent/validator/041-mode-a.json` per the framework's Durable State Rule.

SANITY BASELINE: an independently-authored, fully honest reference implementation (mutants/base_honest.py, written from design_notes without copying the generator's own prototype) was run against all 22 verification entries on the disposable clone and passed all 22 cleanly (including the docstring exit-code-table structural check, entry 4), confirming the suite does not false-fail a correct implementation.

PROBE ISOLATION CONFIRMED: `git status --porcelain` was checked before and after every probe in this review. The real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework shows only the two pre-existing untracked entries (.agent/contracts/041.json, v0.6.0-release-notes.md) throughout, unchanged by this review, aside from this review's own artifact at .agent/qa/041-contract-review.json. All mutant CLIs and adversarial fixtures were built and exercised only against a disposable `git clone` at the session scratchpad (.../scratchpad/041review/clone1, cloned from the real repo at HEAD 17408d942cb09acced7c0874cd4bd363dce3bfc5) and its own ephemeral `mktemp -d` sub-fixtures, per each verification command's own construction; nothing under ecosystem/, schema/, tools/, docs/, NIZAM.json, or any other .agent/ path in the real repository was created, edited, or deleted.

final_verdict.summary: NOT YET APPROVED. Adversarial mutation testing against 22-entry suite confirms the blocking/exception/exit-code mechanics (dirty-tracked-file detection, default-deny untracked handling, missing-reference detection, pending-withholding, operator-approval, blocking-precedence, usage-error discrimination, output isolation) are thoroughly and correctly covered: six independently-constructed adversarial mutants (always-exit-0/copied-fixture; dirty-tracked-state-ignored; blocking-downgraded-under-any-tolerate-flag; exit-code-smuggling-with-fabricated-approval; wrong-usage-error-code; partial-missing-reference-check; plus a bonus output-isolation-leak probe) were each correctly killed by the contracted entries (7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 14, 15, 6 respectively), and a fully honest, independently-authored reference implementation passes all 22 entries cleanly. However, one genuine, high-severity coverage gap was confirmed by adversarial construction: a CLI that implements every blocking/exception/exit-code mechanic honestly but FABRICATES the baseline artifact's content (an unrelated hardcoded SHA instead of the fixture's real git HEAD, a stale unrelated timestamp, and a false 'verified-green' CI claim in place of the honest limitation-note design_notes promises) passes all 22 verification entries unmodified, because schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json validates shape (non-empty revision/timestamp strings) but never truth, and no verification entry independently recomputes and compares against the real repository state. This directly contradicts design_notes' own explicit claims (real-HEAD anchoring; honest ci_references note; 'never fabricating an unlabelled value') -- populated as unsupported_claims and a missing_acceptance_coverage entry, which per the orchestrator's JSON Verdict Parse Rule blocks auto-advance regardless of this final_verdict.approved value. Required fix is narrow and cheap: extend one or two existing verification entries (e.g. 5, 11, or 12) to independently recompute the fixture's real `git rev-parse HEAD` and assert baseline.json's repository/framework reference revision matches it exactly, and to assert the ci_references item carries the promised honest 'note'. Not fit for implementation as-is; resubmit as revision 1 with this fix for a fast re-review.
